title: Enqueue a job that depends on two previous Multi steps
prompt: Write `MyApp.Projects.create_project/2` that takes an `owner_id` and project `attrs`. Use `Ecto.Multi` to: (1) load the owner via `Multi.run(:owner, fn repo, _ -> {:ok, Accounts.get_user!(owner_id)} end)`, (2) insert the project with a changeset built from the attrs and the owner, (3) enqueue `MyApp.Workers.ProjectSetupWorker` in the same transaction using `Oban.insert(:setup_job, fn %{project: project, owner: owner} -> ProjectSetupWorker.new(%{"project_id" => project.id, "owner_id" => owner.id}) end)`. Run the multi with `Repo.transaction/1`. Return `{:ok, project}` or `{:error, reason}`. Write to `lib/my_app/projects.ex`.
tier_rationale: Multi-step Multi with a dependent Oban.insert that reads prior step results. Requires mental model of Multi.run/3 + Oban.insert/3.
